name: Ropa Vieja
recipeInstructions:
1. In a suitable soup kettle, place the meat, bay leaf, whole onion, tomato cut in half, celery rib cut in half, and salt. Cover with cold water and bring to a rapid boil. Skim several times.
2. Lower heat and cook until meat is tender (approximately 1 hour).
3. Allow meat to cool in stock, then remove and shred. Strain the stock and reserve for sauce and soup.
4. TOMATO SAUCE FOR ROPA VIEJA:
a. Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add onion and green pepper and saute until transparent. Add garlic, bay leaf, oregano, and cumin; cook 5 minutes longer.
b. Combine tomato sauce, sugar, vinegar, stock, salt, and wine. Add to the sauteed vegetables. Stir well and bring to a boil.
c. Lower heat and cook for approximately 30 minutes, stirring at intervals.
4. In a 2-1/2 quart casserole with cover, place the shredded meat. Pour sauce over the meat and mix well. Cover and simmer for another 20 to 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Serve over white rice accompanied by very ripe fried plantains, when available.
